hypothesis: When one modality is systematically absent or corrupted during inference (e.g., XRD patterns unavailable for amorphous materials), X-MoPA's cross-modal predictive training will produce latent representations for the missing modality that are more accurate predictors of downstream properties than representations from single-modality baselines trained exclusively on the available modalities, because the joint embedding space encodes inter-modal redundancy that allows reconstruction of missing modality information with sufficient fidelity to preserve property-relevant structure.
